notable_patterns: Comprehensive organization-based multi-tenancy with organizationId fields
Soft delete support via deletedAt timestamps on Zone and Table
Rich relationship modeling with cascading deletes on dependent entities
Financial precision with Decimal(10,2) for currency fields
Polymorphic modeling for OrderItem with product snapshots
JSON fields for flexible metadata and modifiers storage
Extensive indexing strategy for query optimization
Status enums for lifecycle tracking (OrderStatus, TableStatus, OrderItemStatus, etc.)

patterns_discovered.description: All entities include organizationId field to support SaaS multi-tenant architecture
createdAt and updatedAt on all entities; some include deletedAt for soft deletes
Multiple entities use combined fields for uniqueness (e.g., organizationId + number for Table)
Strategic index creation on foreign keys, status fields, and date ranges for filtering
examples: tableId + status for order lookup optimization

patterns_discovered.description: Dependent entities use onDelete: Cascade to maintain referential integrity
OrderItem stores productName and variantName snapshots to preserve order history
patterns_discovered.rationale: Protects against product name/price changes after order placement

patterns_discovered.description: Json fields used for extensible data (metadata, modifiers)
Multiple enum-based status fields tracking entity state transitions
examples: OrderStatus: PENDING, CONFIRMED, PREPARED, DELIVERED, COMPLETED, CANCELLED
OrderItemStatus: PENDING, CONFIRMED, PREPARED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED

gotchas_discovered.description: Table has both currentOrderId (single) and orders[] (many) relations to Order
gotchas_discovered.impact: Must maintain consistency: currentOrder must be in orders array; tableAsCurrent in Order creates bidirectional link
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add validation logic to ensure currentOrderId always references an active order in orders array

gotchas_discovered.description: Multiple nullable foreign keys (customerId, staffId, tableId, zoneId) allow incomplete data
gotchas_discovered.impact: Application must handle null cases for customer orders, unassigned tables, tables without zones
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Document business rules about which combinations are valid (e.g., can takeaway orders have tableId?)

gotchas_discovered.description: orderNumber is unique only within organizationId scope, not globally
gotchas_discovered.impact: Different organizations can have same order numbers; must filter by organizationId in queries
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Ensure application layer always includes organizationId in order number lookups

gotchas_discovered.description: QRCode.tableId is unique, meaning each table can have only one QRCode
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cannot create multiple QR codes per table (e.g., for testing, backup links)
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Clarify if this is intentional; may need to remove @unique to support multi-QR scenarios

gotchas_discovered.description: Feedback.orderId is unique, limiting one feedback per order
gotchas_discovered.impact: Prevents multiple feedback entries per order (e.g., separate food and service ratings)
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Remove @unique if split-rating functionality is needed in future

gotchas_discovered.impact: May not work for non-Turkish restaurants; needs override at creation time
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider making currency configurable per organization or validating against organization settings

gotchas_discovered.description: All monetary fields use Decimal(10, 2) - fixed max of 99,999.99
gotchas_discovered.impact: Large orders (e.g., catering, events) could overflow; no support for fractional cents
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Document limit; consider Decimal(12, 2) or (15, 2) for enterprise use cases

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created comprehensive order.prisma schema file
approach_outcome.completeness: All required entities created: Zone, Table, QRCode, Order, OrderItem, ServiceRequest plus extended entities (Customer, Feedback, LoyaltyPoint, Notification)
approach_outcome.schema_quality: Well-structured with comprehensive documentation, proper indexing strategy, cascading deletes, and multi-tenancy support
approach_outcome.alignment_with_spec: Follows patterns defined in DATABASE_SCHEMA.md section 3.4 with consistent id generation (cuid), audit fields, and relationship modeling

recommendations.recommendation: Add database-level constraints or application validation for currentOrderId consistency - ensure Table.currentOrderId always exists in Table.orders array and is in an active status
recommendations.rationale: Orphaned currentOrderId references could cause UI/query issues

recommendations.recommendation: Document business rules for optional fields: which order types allow null tableId, null customerId, null staffId; when can orders transition between statuses
recommendations.rationale: Prevents data quality issues from ambiguous nullability

recommendations.recommendation: Verify Decimal(10, 2) precision is sufficient for all order sizes; consider Decimal(12, 2) for enterprise safety margin
recommendations.rationale: Prevents overflow errors on large orders or high-volume days

recommendations.recommendation: Remove @unique constraint from QRCode.tableId if multi-QR support needed; remove @unique from Feedback.orderId if detailed rating breakdowns planned
recommendations.rationale: Prevents refactoring pain if requirements change

recommendations.recommendation: Add row-level security (RLS) policies at database level to prevent cross-organization data leaks; verify organizationId filtering in all queries
recommendations.rationale: Critical for SaaS security when handling multiple restaurant organizations

recommendations.recommendation: Make Order.currency configurable per organization instead of defaulting to 'TRY'
Monitor index effectiveness on composite indexes; consider adding index on Order.staffId given staff analytics use cases
recommendations.rationale: Improves performance for staff performance reports and staff-to-order queries
